attributes.content:
# Workflow Predicate System V1 for Golden Events

This document describes the predicate workflow system as it is currently implemented in `round-kiwi`, not the broader future-facing multi-engine proposal.

The current implementation is intentionally narrow:

- one completion engine: deterministic predicates
- one data model for step completion
- one evaluator used by both live mode and replay mode
- one progress formula derived from step weights

This version does not implement:

- LLM-based step completion
- custom TypeScript command execution
- workflow-scoped predicates beyond messages, attachments, and linked cards

Those can come later, but the current system is designed so the deterministic path stands on its own.

---

## Current Status

`round-kiwi` now has a working predicate-only workflow system with these properties:

- workflow steps store a `predicate` and a `weight`
- step completion is derived from workflow facts
- progress percent is derived from completed weight
- replay re-evaluates predicates against a prefix of history
- predicate authoring uses structured fields instead of raw text
- predicate conditions and predicates have computed titles so Boxel does not render them as `Untitled`

This makes the workflow UI materially more self-consistent than the original hand-authored step-status model.

---

## What Is Implemented Now

### 1. Structured predicate fields

The current implementation adds two field types.

```gts
PredicateConditionField {
  subject
  attachmentType
  author
  tone
  textContains
  fieldName
  comparator
  value
  cardTitle
}

PredicateField {
  group
  conditions
  cardTitle
}
```

The current workflow step shape is:

```gts
StepField {
  label
  status     // legacy fallback when no predicate exists
  weight
  predicate
}
```

### 2. Enum-backed dropdowns

The predicate UI uses enum-backed fields for bounded choices.

Current supported enums:

- `group`: `all`, `any`
- `subject`: `attachment`, `linked-card`, `message`
- `comparator`: `present`, `equals`, `contains`
- `attachmentType`: `form`, `survey`, `proposal`, `contract`, `invoice`, `payment`, `workspace`, `quote`, `confirmation`, `profile`
- `tone`: `advisor`, `captain`, `client`, `owner`, `self`

Unbounded inputs remain free text today:

- `author`
- `textContains`
- `fieldName`
- `value`

### 3. Custom embedded and edit UI

Predicate authoring is no longer raw nested JSON.

Implemented authoring behavior:

- `PredicateConditionField.embedded` renders a readable chip-based summary
- `PredicateConditionField.edit` renders a structured editor
- the editor only shows relevant controls for the selected subject
- `PredicateField.embedded` renders the rule group plus embedded condition cards
- `PredicateField.edit` renders rule mode plus editable conditions

### 4. Computed titles

Both conditions and predicates now compute `cardTitle`.

This fixes the Boxel list fallback problem where the UI showed `Untitled Predicate Condition`.

Examples of current computed title shapes:

- `Attachment: proposal`
- `Linked Card: contract.signedBy is present`
- `Message: text contains made her decision`
- `All: Attachment: proposal`
- `All of 2: Linked Card: profile.vendorName is present`

### 5. Deterministic evaluator

The evaluator is implemented directly in `workflow-card.gts`.

It:

- normalizes message facts
- normalizes attachment facts
- follows `linkedCard` from attachments
- evaluates each condition against the selected subject
- applies `all` or `any` across the condition set
- derives workflow step status from predicate truth

### 6. Replay integration

Replay uses the same evaluator as live mode.

The only difference is the fact window:

- live mode evaluates against all messages and attachments
- replay mode evaluates only against the visible prefix of history

Replay also surfaces runtime feedback:

- current derived completion percent
- latest completed step label when a predicate flips to true
- active replay progress text

### 7. Subtle progress animation

The right-side workflow donut now has a subtle animation treatment:

- the ring fill eases when percent changes
- while replay is active, the donut has a very light breathing motion

This is intentionally restrained. It is there to reinforce state change, not become a focal animation.

---

## Predicate Vocabulary in V1

The current predicate system supports three subjects.

### Message predicates

A message condition can match on:

- `author`
- `tone`
- `textContains`

Examples:

```txt
Message where tone is advisor and text includes "terms look good"
Message where text includes "book you"
```

### Attachment predicates

An attachment condition can match on:

- `attachmentType`
- `author`
- `tone`
- `textContains`

Examples:

```txt
Attachment where type is proposal
Attachment where type is payment
```

### Linked-card predicates

A linked-card condition first scopes through an attachment, then checks a field on the linked card.

It can match on:

- attachment context: `attachmentType`, `author`, `tone`, `textContains`
- linked-card field: `fieldName`
- comparison: `present`, `equals`, `contains`
- optional comparison value

Examples:

```txt
Linked Card where type is contract and signedBy is present
Linked Card where type is confirmation and trackingNumber is present
Linked Card where type is payment and amount equals "$2,500.00"
```

### Group semantics

A predicate groups conditions with either:

- `all`: every condition must be true
- `any`: at least one condition must be true

---

## Runtime Evaluation Model

The evaluator works over normalized facts, not directly over the raw JSON structure.

### Message facts

```ts
{
  index,
  author,
  tone,
  text,
  isBot
}
```

### Attachment facts

```ts
{
  index,
  attachmentType,
  typeLabel,
  status,
  messageIndex,
  author,
  tone,
  text,
  linkedCard
}
```

### Evaluation rules

For each step:

1. if the step has a predicate, evaluate it
2. if the step does not have a predicate, fall back to the authored `status`
3. mark all true predicate steps as `completed`
4. mark the first unresolved step as `current`
5. mark the remaining unresolved steps as `upcoming`

This means step state is mostly derived now, but there is still a legacy fallback path for non-migrated steps.

---

## Progress Model

The current implementation derives progress from weights.

```txt
totalWeight = sum(step.weight)
completedWeight = sum(weight of completed steps)
progressPercent = round((completedWeight / totalWeight) * 100)
```

Current behavior:

- if a step has no explicit positive weight, it falls back to `1`
- all Golden Events example workflows currently use `weight = 1` on every step
- the authored `progressPercent` remains in workflow JSON for compatibility, but the UI resolves percent from predicates and weights

This gives a single mathematical source of truth for progress.

---

## Replay Model

Replay is prefix evaluation over the thread.

Current replay logic:

1. initialize replay with `replayVisibleCount = 0`
2. reveal messages one at a time
3. include attachments only when their source message is visible
4. evaluate step predicates against visible messages and visible attachments only
5. update derived step states
6. update derived completion percentage
7. surface any newly completed step labels in the replay pill

This is the core self-consistency property of the system:

- no separate replay-only completion rules
- no separate replay-only percent formula

The same predicate engine drives both modes.

---

## Current Golden Events Mappings

The current Golden Events workflows are now backed by actual predicate data.

These examples describe the system as implemented today, including places where the predicates are still more heuristic than ideal.

### 1. Wedding in Wonderland

Current predicates:

- `Receive request` -> attachment `form`
- `Send proposal` -> attachment `proposal`
- `Sign contract` -> linked-card field `signedBy` on `contract`
- `Receive deposit` -> attachment `payment`
- `Active planning` -> attachment `workspace`
- `Wedding day` -> unresolved message-text predicate
- `Final payment` -> unresolved linked-card `payment.amount == $2,500.00`

Current consequence:

- the workflow resolves to `71%`
- `Active planning` completes deterministically
- the final two steps remain unresolved

### 2. Vendor Agreement with The Gorgeous Hotel

Current predicates:

- `Review terms` -> advisor message contains `terms look good`
- `Sign contract` -> linked-card field `signedBy` on `contract`
- `Submit vendor profile` -> linked-card fields `vendorName` and `bio` on `profile`
- `Listed on website` -> captain message contains `added you to our website`

Current consequence:

- the workflow resolves to `100%`
- this is functionally correct today
- `Listed on website` still depends on message text instead of a dedicated listing artifact

### 3. Save-the-Dates

Current predicates:

- `Choose vendor` -> owner message contains `Monday should be doable`
- `Write content` -> message contains `last-minute edits`
- `Finalize design` -> message contains `final design`
- `Place order` -> attachment `confirmation`
- `Track delivery` -> linked-card field `trackingNumber` on `confirmation`
- `Send payment` -> unresolved attachment `payment`

Current consequence:

- the workflow resolves to `83%`
- shipping is now deterministic because it has a linked card with tracking data
- the earlier creative/approval steps still rely on message text and should eventually move to explicit evidence cards

### 4. DJ Booking - Jimmy Joe

Current predicates:

- `Request quote` -> attachment `quote`
- `Compare quotes` -> advisor message contains `made her decision`
- `Client decision` -> message contains `made her decision`
- `Book DJ` -> message contains `book you`
- `Pay deposit` -> message contains `All done, here's the receipt`
- `Confirm details` -> unresolved message-text predicate

Current consequence:

- the workflow resolves to `83%`
- the workflow is live on deterministic predicates today
- several steps are still driven by message phrasing rather than explicit decision or booking artifacts

---

## What Is Good About the Current System

The current predicate system already delivers several important wins.

### 1. Progress is now mathematically consistent

The percent shown in the UI is derived from the same source of truth as the steps.

### 2. Replay is a proof of consistency

As history unfolds, the evaluator checks off steps using the same rules used in live mode.

### 3. Predicate authoring is meaningfully better

Users can now:

- see a readable predicate summary
- edit conditions through structured controls
- avoid raw JSON as the primary authoring experience

### 4. Boxel list rendering is improved

Computed titles on predicates and conditions eliminate `Untitled` fallback labels.

---

## Known Gaps

The system is working, but several parts are still intentionally incomplete.

### 1. Attachments still use `messageRef` indexes

This is the biggest data-model weakness.

Current state:

- attachments still point to messages by stringified index
- replay and predicate evaluation still depend on message order stability

Recommended next step:

- add stable message IDs
- store `messageId` on attachments
- keep `messageOrder` only as a convenience for replay

### 2. Linked-card field names are still free text

Current state:

- `fieldName` is a plain string field
- the editor cannot validate that a field name is valid for the selected attachment/card type

Recommended next step:

- dynamically narrow `fieldName` options from the linked card type
- use enum-backed options where possible

### 3. Some predicates still rely on message text

Current state:

- several Golden Events steps use `textContains`
- this is deterministic, but more brittle than explicit evidence cards

Recommended next step:

- add explicit evidence cards for approvals, decisions, bookings, listings, and selections

### 4. `status` and `progressPercent` still exist in source JSON

Current state:

- the UI mostly derives state
- legacy authored values still remain in the stored workflow data

Recommended next step:

- remove them as canonical authored values once migration is complete

### 5. There is not yet a dedicated step authoring editor

Current state:

- predicate authoring is improved
- step authoring as a whole is not yet presented as a custom unified field

Recommended next step:

- give `StepField` its own edit UI so `label`, `weight`, and `predicate` are edited together

---

## Recommended Next Moves

The current implementation is good enough to extend, but the next work should raise the quality of evidence rather than broaden the engine surface.

### Priority 1

Replace `messageRef` index addressing with stable message IDs.

### Priority 2

Add dynamic `fieldName` narrowing for linked-card predicates.

### Priority 3

Introduce explicit evidence cards where message-text predicates are carrying too much semantic weight.

### Priority 4

Build a custom `StepField` edit UI that wraps:

- step label
- step weight
- predicate editor
- optional helper text

### Priority 5

Only after the deterministic system is stable, revisit whether LLM or command-based checks are still needed.

---

## Final Recommendation

Treat the current predicate system as the base workflow engine for Boxel-style threaded workflows.

It already has the right core properties:

- structured and editable as card data
- deterministic
- replay-compatible
- progress-consistent
- explainable in UI
- composable as a shared custom field

The next step is not to add a second engine.

The next step is to strengthen the evidence model so more workflows can be expressed without brittle message-text matching.

cardInfo.name: Workflow Predicate System V1
cardInfo.summary: Current implemented state of the predicate-only workflow system in round-kiwi, including structured predicate fields, replay-driven evaluation, derived weighted progress, and current Golden Events mappings.
